businessSummary.oneLiner: World's largest EV maker and a 'Physical AI Platform' spanning autonomous driving, robotics, energy, and AI chips
businessSummary.description: Tesla goes beyond EV sales with FSD autonomous driving, Cybercab robotaxis, Optimus humanoid robots, and Megapack energy storage. FY2025 revenue was $94.8B with GAAP EPS of $1.08, and Q4 2025 gross margin hit 20.1%, a two-year high. FSD transitioned to a $99/month subscription model to maximize recurring revenue. Key catalysts in April 2026 include Cybercab production start, Roadster reveal, and FSD Netherlands approval.
howTheyMakeMoney: Energy storage and generation (Megapack, Powerwall, Solar)
Services and charging (Supercharger network, insurance, OTA upgrades)

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Over 16x the S&P 500 average (22x). Extreme premium vs auto sector average (10x), but reflects Tesla's AI/robotics/energy platform potential

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E (2026E)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on consensus EPS of $2.25. Still 8.5x the S&P 500 forward P/E (20x), but half of the TTM P/E, reflecting expected earnings improvement

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Growth (FY2025)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Slight revenue decline YoY. 2026 consensus projects +15% ($108.9B) rebound, though UBS cut Q1 delivery estimate to 345K (-18% QoQ)

keyMetrics.name: Gross Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Recovered to 20.1% in Q4 2025, a two-year high, but still below 2022 levels (25.6%)

keyMetrics.name: Operating Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Down from 7.24% in FY2024 due to pricing competition and R&D investment expansion

keyMetrics.name: FCF (Free Cash Flow)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Significant improvement in cash generation. Comfortable cash position despite massive investments in Terafab and Cybercab production

growthDrivers.title: Wedbush Calls Terafab a 'Game Changer' (3/25)
growthDrivers.description: Wedbush reiterated Outperform and $600 target, calling the Terafab semiconductor factory a 'game changer' for Tesla's AI strategy. AI5 chip small-batch production expected late 2026, volume production in 2027

growthDrivers.title: European Sales Rebound After 13-Month Slump (Feb +11.8%)
growthDrivers.description: ACEA data shows February European registrations up 11.8% YoY, first increase since December 2024. However, BYD grew over 100% in the same period, intensifying market share competition

growthDrivers.title: Terafab $20-25B Joint Chip Factory (3/22)
growthDrivers.description: Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI are jointly investing in a Terafab semiconductor facility in Austin, Texas. Plans to produce vehicle chips and AI satellite chips in-house, targeting 2027 production

growthDrivers.title: Cybercab Production + Roadster Reveal in April
growthDrivers.description: Steerless, fully autonomous Cybercab robotaxi production begins in April alongside the next-gen Roadster reveal. Two major catalysts concentrated in a single month

growthDrivers.title: Netherlands FSD Approval Expected by 4/10
growthDrivers.description: Tesla expects FSD Supervised approval from Dutch authorities by April 10. If approved, summer rollout across the EU is possible

growthDrivers.title: China Sales Surge (+35% YoY) + $2.9B Solar Equipment
growthDrivers.description: January-February 2026 China sales up 35% YoY. Also negotiating $2.9B in solar equipment purchases from Chinese firms for 100GW domestic solar capacity

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 50%+ US EV market share, 8.4 billion FSD miles, NACS Supercharger standard dominance, Terafab for AI chip verticalization

moats.description: 8.4 billion real-world FSD miles. An insurmountable autonomous driving dataset that competitors cannot replicate in the short term
Supercharger network adopted as NACS standard. Ford, GM, and other competitors now use Tesla's charging infrastructure
Battery cells, AI chips (Terafab), software, and charging infrastructure all in-house. Cost advantages and faster innovation cycles
Synonymous with EVs. High brand recognition and passionate fanbase create demand without marketing spend

competitors.name: BYD
competitors.detail: China market leader with 100%+ European growth. Price competitiveness advantage rapidly closing the gap with Tesla

competitors.name: Zoox (Amazon)
competitors.detail: Expanding robotaxi service to SF, Las Vegas, Austin, and Miami. Direct robotaxi competitor

competitors.name: Waymo (Alphabet)
competitors.detail: Ahead of Tesla in autonomous driving commercialization. Expanding robotaxi services

competitors.name: Hyundai/Kia
competitors.detail: Expanding global EV market share with Ioniq and EV6. Competing on value and quality

recentNews.headline: Wedbush Calls Terafab a 'Game Changer,' Reiterates $600 Target
recentNews.significance: Wedbush sees Terafab as central to Tesla's AI strategy. AI5 chip small-batch production in late 2026, volume production in 2027. Bull conviction intact

recentNews.headline: Longtime Bull Trip Chowdhry Flips to Sell, Sets $150 Target
recentNews.significance: Historically bullish analyst cites AI narrative collapse as reason for downgrade. $150 target implies 60% downside from current levels

recentNews.headline: European February Registrations Up 11.8%, First Rebound in 13 Months
recentNews.significance: Year-long European sales decline finally reversed. However, BYD also grew 100%+, matching Tesla's market share at 1.8%

recentNews.headline: Terafab: Tesla, SpaceX, xAI Joint $20-25B Chip Factory
recentNews.significance: Austin-based in-house semiconductor factory announced. Vehicle and AI satellite chip production targeted for 2027

recentNews.headline: NHTSA Escalates FSD Probe to 'Engineering Analysis' for 3.2M Vehicles
recentNews.significance: Investigation upgraded one level. Increased recall probability could impact Cybercab licensing and overall autonomous strategy

analystOpinions.notableComment: Wedbush sees Terafab as a 'game changer' and maintains $600. Trip Chowdhry flips to Sell at $150. Extreme polarization with 10 of 34 analysts rating Sell

risks.title: Bull Defection Signals Deteriorating Sentiment
risks.description: Even longtime bulls like Trip Chowdhry are flipping to Sell. 10 of 34 analysts now rate Sell, an unusually high proportion signaling a clear shift in Wall Street sentiment

risks.title: Extreme Valuation Premium
risks.description: P/E of 356x and P/S of 14.6x are extreme by any benchmark. Significant downside risk if earnings disappoint

risks.title: Q1 Delivery Forecast Slashed (UBS: 345K)
risks.description: UBS cut Q1 delivery estimate to 345K (-7% vs consensus of 371K), an 18% QoQ decline suggesting demand softening is materializing

risks.title: NHTSA FSD Probe Escalated to Engineering Analysis
risks.description: Federal investigation covering 3.2M vehicles upgraded. Higher recall probability could derail the entire autonomous strategy

risks.title: Robotaxi Competition + Macro Headwinds
risks.description: Zoox expanding to 4 cities, Waymo growing. Oil at $91 and S&P 500 decline adding macro pressure

buyReasons.title: April Catalyst Concentration
buyReasons.rationale: Cybercab production start, Roadster reveal, and Netherlands FSD approval (4/10) all converging in April. Multiple major events in a compressed timeframe

buyReasons.title: European and Chinese Sales Rebounding Simultaneously
buyReasons.rationale: Europe's first rebound in 13 months (+11.8%) and China +35% YoY. Two key markets returning to growth at the same time

buyReasons.title: Terafab + Energy Expansion
buyReasons.rationale: Wedbush calls it a 'game changer.' $20-25B chip factory plus $2.9B solar equipment accelerating business diversification

buyReasons.title: FCF Surge (+74%)
buyReasons.rationale: Free cash flow of $6.22B, up 74% YoY. Strong cash generation despite massive investment commitments

overallOpinion: Wedbush calls Terafab a 'game changer' and holds $600, while longtime bull Trip Chowdhry flips to Sell at $150. With 10 of 34 analysts on Sell, Wall Street is as divided on Tesla as it has ever been.

April brings Cybercab production, Roadster reveal, and Netherlands FSD approval (4/10) all at once. European (+11.8%) and Chinese (+35%) sales are rebounding simultaneously, offering early signs of an earnings recovery.

Trading below both the 50-day ($389) and 200-day ($402) SMAs with RSI at 35, approaching oversold territory. At $384.68 and YTD -15%, April events could trigger a sharp rebound, but failure risks a drop below $350.

investmentVerdict.summary: Wall Street is extremely polarized: Wedbush at $600 vs Chowdhry at $150. With Cybercab, Roadster, and FSD European approval all concentrated in April, waiting for results before positioning is prudent. At 356x P/E, new entries carry significant risk.

sources.name: Investing.com
sources.description: Wedbush Terafab 'game changer' call, $600 target reiterated

sources.name: CoinCentral
sources.description: AI5 chip timeline: small batch late 2026, volume 2027

sources.name: Reuters
sources.description: European February sales rebound and BYD competition

sources.name: MEXC
sources.description: March 25 price analysis and valuation assessment

threads.hook: Wedbush $600 vs Chowdhry $150: extreme Wall Street polarization

April is stacked: Cybercab production, Roadster reveal, Netherlands FSD approval. Europe +11.8% and China +35% are early signs of sales recovery.

Below 50-day ($389) and 200-day ($402). RSI 35. Target $407 (+5.8%). April events will define the direction.

witty.text:

fact.hook: Wedbush $600 vs Chowdhry $150, Wall Street divided
fact.text:
$TSLA (3/25) $384.68

Wedbush Terafab 'game changer' $600 maintained. Chowdhry Sell at $150. 10 of 34 on Sell.

Europe +11.8%, China +35%. April Cybercab, Roadster, FSD Netherlands. UBS Q1 345K cut. Below 50-day ($389), 200-day ($402). RSI 35. Target $407 (+5.8%).

witty.hook: $600 and $150: a 4x spread on the same ticker
witty.text:
$TSLA (3/25) $384.68

Wedbush says $600, Chowdhry says $150. One sees a game-changing AI chip factory, the other sees a collapsing narrative. Both can't be right. April will be the judge.

Terafab ships AI5 chips in 2027. Cybercab starts in April. Europe and China sales are recovering. But UBS cut Q1 deliveries. Classic Tesla: the bull and bear case are equally compelling.

RSI 35, YTD -15%. Target $407 (+5.8%). This is the kind of stock that makes you question everything.

empathy.hook: When analysts disagree by 4x, holding gets stressful
empathy.text:
$TSLA (3/25) $384.68

When one analyst says $600 and another says $150 on the same stock, it's natural to feel lost. The uncertainty is real and valid.

The positives: Europe rebounds, China +35%, April is packed with catalysts. Terafab could reshape the company long-term.

The concerns: RSI 35, UBS delivery cuts, NHTSA probe. Target $407 (+5.8%). April will bring answers. Sometimes patience is the best strategy.
